key_insight: The paper shows that conforming spectral Galerkin discretisations can produce certified lower eigenvalue bounds, not just highly accurate approximations, by using a projection-based enclosure framework.
engineering_relevance: This enables rigorous two-sided spectral bounds for Schrödinger-type operators with far fewer degrees of freedom than certified finite elements, improving efficiency for quantum and wave-physics computations.
method: Derive lower eigenvalue bounds from a projection constant for spectral Galerkin trial spaces.
Use a projection-gap estimate for $-\Delta+V$ to obtain explicit constants for the standard Galerkin matrix.
Add a composite discretisation and Neumann truncation to remove or reduce dependence on $\|V\|_{L^\infty}$ and certify bounds on $\mathbb{R}^d$.
evidence_signal: For two benchmark potentials on $\mathbb{R}^2$, the spectral enclosures matched or exceeded certified finite element bounds while using about two orders of magnitude fewer degrees of freedom.
watch_item: The guarantees rely on specific projection constants, domain truncation, and problem structure; extending them to more general or highly singular potentials may require additional analysis.
paper_title: Guaranteed Lower Eigenvalue Bounds for Spectral Galerkin Methods with Application to Schrödinger Operators
